trigger: Tag added (from Skool free group join via calculator opt-in)

emails.subject: Here's your calculator (plus something extra)
emails.body_text:
Hey
Your tyre pressure calculator is ready and waiting for you. But before I hand it over, let me tell you what you've actually walked into. Because it's more than a calculator. Here's how to access your calculator:
Step 1: Head to the Roadman Cycling Clubhouse (here's the link to the Clubhouse)
Step 2: If you haven't already, create your free Skool account. Takes 30 seconds.
Step 3: Once you're inside, go to the Classroom tab.
Step 4: You'll see the Tyre Pressure Calculator right there. Open it, plug in your numbers, and you're away.

Now here's the thing most people miss. The community you just joined isn't a ghost town with a calculator in it. There are riders in there right now talking about real stuff. Routes. Kit. Racing. Training. The kind of conversations you actually want to have.
The kind of conversations which progress your fitness & motivation. And here's what makes it interesting: the more you show up and contribute, the more you unlock.
Skool has a built-in levelling system. Every time you post, comment, or help someone out, you earn points. As you level up, you unlock rewards that I've set up specifically for this community.
You unlock Level 1 by joining
At Level 2, you'll get a Free Core Training Guide
At Level 3, you'll get a Free Nutrition Masterclass with World Tour Nutritionists
At Level 4, I've built training plans you can import into your Training Peaks account
At Level 5, you'll get 3 amazing cycling-specific cookbooks.

So don't just grab the calculator and disappear. Have a look around. Introduce yourself.
My goal with this community is to give you all the tools you need to succeed in cycling, for free.

Talk tomorrow.
Anthony
Roadman Cycling

P.S. If you have any issues accessing the calculator or the community, just reply to this email. I read everyone.
Here's the link to join again skool.com/roadman

emails.subject: Most people miss this inside the community
emails.preheadline: You're sitting on more than you think
emails.body_text:
Hey
Quick one today. Yesterday, you grabbed the tyre pressure calculator. Nice. But I want to make sure you don't miss what else is sitting inside the community, because most people join, grab the thing they came for, and never look around. That would be a mistake. Inside the Classroom section, alongside your calculator, you'll find free resources that I've put together from over 1,300 podcast episodes and years of conversations with some of the sharpest minds in cycling. We're talking training fundamentals, nutrition basics, bike setup guides, the kind of stuff that would cost you hundreds if you hired someone to walk you through it.
I've just added this conversation with one of the best bike fitters in the world, Derral Fitzgerald & we created a bike fitting guide which is also free so you can action his recommendations. But here's what I think is even more valuable than the resources.
The feed. Right now there are riders in there posting questions, sharing what's working, debating the stuff that actually matters.
You know that feeling when you're training alone and you're not sure if you're doing it right? That disappears when you're surrounded by people having the same conversations. So here's what I'd suggest: go into the community today, find one post that interests you, and drop a comment. That's it. One comment.
You'll earn your first points toward levelling up, and you might start a conversation that changes how you think about something.

Here's the link to join us www.skool.com/roadman
Talk tomorrow.
Anthony

emails.subject: How to eat like Tom Pidcock
emails.preheadline: They work with 40% of World Tour riders
emails.body_text:
Hey
I've been running the Roadman community long enough to notice a pattern. The people who get the most value aren't the ones who consume the most content. They're the ones who engage. They ask questions. They answer other people's questions. They share what they're learning.
And something interesting happens when they do that. They learn faster. Not a little bit. Noticeably faster. There's a reason for that. When you have to articulate what you know, you cement it. When you help someone solve a problem, you sharpen your own understanding. That's why I built the level-up system the way I did.

Every time you post, comment, like, or help someone out, you earn points. As those points stack up, you level up. And each level unlocks something new. When you get to level 3 (which doesn't take much work)
You'll unlock a Masterclass I've recorded with Dr Sam Impey & Dr David Dunne. These are the two guys who are responsible for the Nutrition strategies of over 40% of WorldTour riders, including guys like Tom Pidcock. I'll show you what to eat, when to eat and how much to eat to optimise your performance & weight loss.

All you need to unlock this is get to level 3 by dropping some comments. This isn't gamification for the sake of it. It's a system that rewards the exact behaviour that makes you better at cycling. The people at the top of the leaderboard aren't just earning badges. They're the ones making the most progress on the bike. So if you haven't introduced yourself yet, today's the day. Tell people where you're based, what you ride, and what you're working on. You'll be surprised how many people are in a similar boat.

[INTRODUCE YOURSELF $†’] here
See you in there.
Anthony

ps. if you haven't joined the free community yet it takes about 30 seconds. Here's the link www.skool.com/roadman

emails.subject: Why most riders plateau (it's not effort)
emails.preheadline: No one wants to talk about this
emails.body_text:
Hey
I need to be straight with you about something today. The free community you're in is good. The resources are real. The conversations are valuable. I wouldn't have built it if I didn't believe that. But there's a ceiling.

And I'd be doing you a disservice if I didn't tell you where it is. Here's what I've seen over 1,300 podcast episodes and years working with riders at every level: the number one reason amateurs plateau has nothing to do with effort. It's not that they're not training hard enough. It's not that they don't care.

It's that they're operating with pieces of a puzzle instead of the full picture. They've got a training plan but no fueling strategy. Or they're eating well but doing zero strength work. Or they're doing everything except managing recovery. So they push hard, feel good for a while, then stall. Or worse, they break down. Sound familiar? When I talk to WorldTour coaches, elite riders, sports scientists, and performance directors, one thing is obvious. Elite athletes never train in isolation. They operate inside a complete performance system - the 5 pillars of performance
where training, nutrition, strength, recovery, and accountability all work together. Not as optional extras. As non-negotiables. That's the gap I built "Not Done Yet" to close.

Not Done Yet is our performance community for riders who want the full system. Not a training plan alone. Not a generic coaching subscription. A performance operating system built on five integrated pillars. You'll get to train like the best in the world do. Over the next few days, I'm going to walk you through exactly what that means and why it works. No pressure. Just clarity. But if you're already curious, you can take a look right now. We offer a 7-day free trial to everyone in our free group so you can see the inside before you commit to anything.

https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling/about
Talk tomorrow.
Anthony
Roadman Cycling

emails.subject: Why your training plan isn't working (even if it's a good one)
emails.preheadline: The training mistake almost every amateur makes
emails.body_text:
Hey
Most riders I talk to have a training plan or use some coaching app. Some of them even have a good one. Structured intervals, progressive overload, the right mix of endurance and intensity. On paper, it looks solid. And yet they're not getting faster. Here's why. A training plan in isolation is like a recipe without a kitchen. The ingredients might be perfect, but if the oven's broken, you're not cooking anything.

Inside Not Done Yet, training is the first pillar. But it doesn't stand alone. It's structured, progressive, and adapted to the individual. The focus isn't on chasing fatigue. It's on creating repeatable adaptation. Right intensity distribution. Right amount of overload. Right timing of recovery. Training that fits your life, not the other way around.

You're not trying to win month 1. You're trying to be strong in the moments that actually matter. Peaking for your key goals (even if that's just health) But here's the part most people miss. Even perfect training falls apart without proper fueling. And that's the second pillar. Most amateurs underfuel without realising it. Others over-restrict and destroy the very adaptation they're trying to create. Inside Not Done Yet, nutrition isn't about dieting. It's about supporting training. Daily fueling structure. In-session fueling. Body composition without performance loss.
Exact macros for every meal based on your prescribed training session. Training and nutrition are the first two pillars of Not Done Yet. When they work together, everything changes. When one is missing, the other can't do its job. That's why it's a system, not a plan.

If you want to see how all five pillars fit together, the 7-day free trial gives you full access. No guesswork. The exact systems and sessions the best riders in the world use, adapted for everyday Joe's & Jane's, like me & you.

[START YOUR 7-DAY FREE TRIAL $†’] https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling
More tomorrow.
Anthony

here's that link again https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling

emails.subject: The two things that will keep you riding at 55
emails.preheadline: What breaks first isn't your engine. It's your chassis.
emails.body_text:
Hey
Let me paint a picture you've probably seen. Rider in their 40s. Fit. Motivated. Training consistently. Then a knee flares up. Or a back issue creeps in. Or they get sick for the third time in four months. And suddenly all that fitness they built just evaporates.

This isn't bad luck. It's predictable. And it's almost always because two pillars were missing. Strength and conditioning.

After 35, strength is not optional. But random gym work doesn't help cycling. In some cases, it actively interferes with it. Inside Not Done Yet, strength work is cyclist-specific, progressive, matched to training load, and designed around injury prevention and long-term durability. Strength supports your cycling. It never competes with it. Recovery. This is the pillar most people ignore until something breaks. And I'm not talking about foam rolling and ice baths. I'm talking about understanding fatigue versus fitness. Knowing when to push and when to pull back. Integrating sleep, stress, travel, and life into your training decisions so you're building over months and years. That's how you're still improving at 50, at 55, at 60. Not by training harder. By being smarter about what surrounds the training. These two pillars are the difference between riders who keep getting better and riders who keep getting injured. The results of the community have blown my mind. You can see for yourself in the videos Aidan dropped 23 kg Matt went from Cat 4 to Cat 2 in weeks I've setup a 7 day free trial (195 usd per month after that) so you can see & feel these changes in your own body
7-day free trial

Chat tomorrow
Anthony

ps here's the link for the 7 day trial www.skool.com

emails.subject: The most underestimated performance tool in cycling
emails.preheadline: This is the pillar that surprises everyone
emails.body_text:
Hey
Training. Nutrition. Strength. Recovery. If those were the only four pillars, Not Done Yet would already be better than most programs out there. But there's a fifth. And honestly, it might be the most important one. Community.

I know. You didn't sign up for a group hug. Hear me out. Training alone works for a while. You're motivated, you're disciplined, you get after it. But eventually one of two things happens. Either motivation fades, or doubt creeps in. Am I doing this right? Is this normal? Should I be further along by now? When you're training in isolation, those questions just loop in your head. Inside Not Done Yet, you're surrounded by serious riders at different levels, all asking good questions, all learning together. There's accountability. Shared learning. Context. Perspective. Someone's already been through what you're dealing with and can tell you exactly what they did.

This replaces guesswork with clarity. And it's something no ai training plan or PDF or YouTube video can give you. That's the fifth pillar. And it's what turns Not Done Yet from a program into something that actually changes how you train, think, and ride.

On top of the five pillars, you get daily training guidance, education around why you're doing what you're doing, access to live Q&As, masterclasses with world-class experts, and ongoing refinement rather than static plans. You're not handed a plan and left alone. You're taught how to think like a coached athlete. That's the real value. If you want to experience what it's like from the inside, the 7-day free trial is there. Full access. No restrictions. See it for yourself. The cost is 195 usd (167 euro) per month after that.

emails.subject: Is Not Done Yet for you? (Honest answer inside)
emails.preheadline: This isn't for everyone. Here's how to know.
emails.body_text:
Hey
I'm not going to pretend Not Done Yet is for everyone. It isn't. If you're looking for a quick fix, a 6-week shred, or someone to just hand you a plan and disappear, this isn't it. There are plenty of places that sell that. They're easy to find. Not Done Yet is for a specific kind of person. It's for the rider who knows their best chapter is still ahead of them. Who isn't chasing shortcuts. Who cares about doing things properly and wants to understand the process. It's not age-specific. It's mindset-specific.

Inside you'll find masters racers, gravel and ultra riders, returning racers who stepped away and are building back, high-level amateurs who want to go further, and people who are balancing serious training with jobs, families, and real life. The common thread is one belief: I'm not done yet. I've more to give.

If that's you, the 7-day free trial (195 usd per month after that) lets you see the inside. Walk around. Read the discussions. Look at the resources. See if the people in there feel like your kind of people.

[TRY IT FREE FOR 7 DAYS $†’] https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling/about
Anthony

Ps. I just added a really nice conversation with Ineos & GB Bike Fit Physio Phil Burt Where we talk about setting up your bike perfectly. It's inside under "Academy"

emails.subject: Here's exactly what's behind the door
emails.preheadline: No fluff. Just what you get.
emails.body_text:
Hey
I've spent the last few days explaining the philosophy behind Not Done Yet. Today I want to get concrete. Here's what you actually get when you walk through the door. You get a performance system built on five integrated pillars: training, nutrition, strength, recovery, and community. Not as separate add-ons. As one connected framework where each piece supports the others. You get daily training sessions built & planned by me in your training peaks account. An ongoing, evolving, fully personalised training plan that adapts to where you are and what you need.

You get one on one coaching calls, access to weekly live Q&As where you can ask real questions and get real answers. You get masterclasses with world-class experts, inside we have extracts from Prof Stephen Seiler, John Wakefield & Dan Lorang (Bora RedBull), Phil Burt (Ineos), & Dr David Dunne (Hexis)
The same people I've interviewed across 1,300+ episodes of the Roadman Podcast. Their collective wisdom informing your training. You get a room full of serious riders who are on the same path. People who will push you, support you, and make the whole thing sustainable in a way that training alone never is.

And you get ongoing refinement. Not a static plan. A living system that evolves as you do. Most programs sell motivation, a plan, and a promise. Not Done Yet builds understanding, structure, and consistency. That's the difference.

The 7-day free trial gives you full access to all of it. No restrictions, no limited version, no bait and switch. You see everything. Then you decide.

emails.subject: I'm not done yet. Are you?
emails.body_text:
Hey
Over the last ten days, you've had access to the free community, the tyre pressure calculator, the resources, and the conversations. I hope you've found value in all of it. I've also been honest with you about what free resources can and can't do. They can give you pieces. What they can't give you is the full system. Not Done Yet exists because I saw a gap. Elite athletes don't improve by training in isolation. They improve because every performance lever is aligned. Training, nutrition, strength, recovery, and the people around them. All of it working together. Most amateurs never get that. Not because they can't. Because nobody packages it for them in a way that fits real life.

That's what Not Done Yet does. If you're the kind of person who believes their best chapter is still ahead, who wants to understand the process and not just follow instructions, who is tired of guesswork and ready for clarity, then this was built for you. The 7-day free trial is still there. Full access. No catches. Try it, see the inside, and decide for yourself.
[START YOUR 7-DAY FREE TRIAL $†’] https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling/about

And if now isn't the right time? That's fine. You're still part of the free community. Keep showing up, keep levelling up, keep learning. The door to Not Done Yet isn't going anywhere. But if you know you're ready, don't wait for the perfect moment. There isn't one. There's just the decision to start. I'm not done yet. Are you?
https://www.skool.com/roadmancycling/about
